behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the test functions (TestConcurrentAppendSameLabels, TestStripeSeries_SetUnlessAlreadySet, TestSetUnlessAlreadySetConcurrentSameLabels, TestSetUnlessAlreadySetConcurrentGC), the file paths (tsdb/agent/db.go, tsdb/agent/series.go), and the required method name (SetUnlessAlreadySet is implied via 'check-then-insert' but the exact name is referenced by tests).

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what's broken and behavioral requirements without specifying exact line changes or the method name verbatim (describes behavior of the new method).

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_setunlessalreadyset_method_exists greps for the literal string 'SetUnlessAlreadySet' in series.go, and multiple tests require specific test function names like TestStripeSeries_SetUnlessAlreadySet — alternative valid fixes using different naming would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute go test, go build, go vet, go mod verify as subprocesses to verify actual behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests run actual Go tests that exercise concurrent append behavior; stubs would not pass race-condition tests.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover the concurrent append behavior, the new method, GC deadlock safety, and signature change via build.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are named clearly with docstrings labeling fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass.

no_typos.reason: No typos in filenames or commands.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the files to modify but this is part of the task scope, not buggy-line localization.
